home.name: Shreya Kakachery
home.tagline: Jack of All Trades, Master of Data Science
home.bio: Curiosity killed the cat, but satisfaction brought it back. Likewise, my goal is to expand my perspective, to live, learn, and grow. Experience is my teacher.

experience.description: Monitored computer-based examinations, providing technical troubleshooting and maintaining a calm, secure testing environment for students under high stress.
Served on the Master of Data Science Admissions Committee, assisting with the evaluation and selection of incoming candidate applications.
Acted as the cohort spokesperson by gathering student feedback through surveys, presenting findings to faculty, and organizing social events.
Managed a multilingual medical terminology database of over 14,000 terms across 10 languages, coordinating with global translators to verify data accuracy.
Facilitated communication between patients and medical professionals in Hindi, Tamil, Malayalam, and English, and translated clinical research documents.
Conducted independent research on ADHD and creativity using novel experimental paradigms, and analyzed fMRI data to map functional neural connectivity.
Instructed elementary, high school, and university students in English comprehension and provided specialized coaching for the IELTS exam.
Tutored university students in chemistry, biology, statistics, neuroscience, and psychology. Adapted teaching methods to diverse learning styles.
Evaluated student coursework and provided constructive, tailored feedback to support academic improvement and comprehension.
Led an independent research project investigating Theory of Mind and autism, programming novel psychological paradigms.
Maintained the association's website and designed promotional graphics and social media campaigns to increase event engagement.
Independently structured and led group martial arts classes for children aged 5 to 12, fostering physical fitness and focus.

projects.title: Portfolio
projects.description: My personal website developed to showcase my academic credentials, professional experience, and technical projects.
projects.why: To showcase my skills and technical projects.

projects.title: Evaluating VLMs for PASER-Based Road Assessment
projects.description: Explores the reliability of Vision-Language Models for assessing asphalt pavement conditions using PASER guidelines from imagery.
projects.why: MDS capstone project in collaboration with PavePal Technologies

projects.title: Paper Plain
projects.description: An accessible web platform dedicated to open-source science communication.
projects.why: Academic papers are very dense, I want to make science accessible.

projects.title: Convene
projects.description: Meeting in the middle made easy. A web app that suggests fair meetup locations using real Metro Vancouver transit data from TransLink.
projects.why: To answer the question 'Where should we meet?'

projects.title: Sudoku
projects.description: A clean, interactive web-based Sudoku game.

projects.title: Layoff Lens
projects.description: A Python-based Shiny dashboard visualizing hiring and layoff trends from 2000 to 2025.
projects.why: My first time making a Shiny dashboard

projects.title: Layoff Lens in R
projects.description: An R Shiny dashboard tracking hiring and layoff trends across America from 2000 to 2025.
projects.why: Convert a Python dashbord to an R Shiny dashboard

projects.title: Trecento
projects.description: A minimalist word counter application featuring an integrated stopwatch.
projects.why: To see if I could make a word counter website

projects.title: Watercolour Map of UBC Campus
projects.description: An interactive, watercolor-styled map of the UBC campus built with Leaflet.js.
projects.why: My first time working with geospatial data

projects.title: MediAction
projects.description: An interactive website designed to visualize the mechanisms and actions of drugs.
projects.why: To visualize drug progression in the human body

projects.title: Multilingual Medical Terminology Website
projects.description: A multilingual website integrating a database of medical terminology translated across 10 different languages.
projects.why: A project for Volentia Translation Services

projects.title: OneVoice
projects.description: A live translation tool optimized for accented English speech. Winner of the BrainStation Microsoft Hackathon!
projects.why: Live transcription if often incorrect for accented English speech, so we wanted to fix that

projects.title: Geospatial Toolkit
projects.description: A custom Python package built for efficient geospatial data manipulation.
projects.why: My first time making a Python package
